README.html.title: EasySave – Logiciel de sauvegarde | EasySaveDocs
README.html.summary: EasySave – Logiciel de sauvegarde Projet fil rouge – ProSoft Groupe (5 membres) : Noa PIERRÉ - Thomas DE CALUWE - Iyas AL HARIRI - Nathan AURICCHIO - Clément MOURGUE 📌 Contexte du projet Ce projet s’inscrit dans le cadre de l’intégration de l’équipe au sein de l’éditeur de logiciels ProSoft, sous la responsabilité du DSI. L’objectif est de concevoir et développer EasySave, un logiciel de sauvegarde professionnel, en respectant des contraintes fortes de qualité, de maintenabilité et de gestion des versions. Le projet se déroule sous la forme de livrables successifs, simulant un cycle de vie logiciel accéléré avec versions majeures et mineures. 🗂️ Calendrier & Livrables 🔹 Livrable 0 & Livrable 1 – EasySave v1.0 (évalué) Jour 1 : Lancement du projet Cahier des charges – version 1.0 Jour 3 : Mise en place de l’environnement de travail Invitation du tuteur sur le dépôt GitHub Veille du livrable 1 : Livraison des diagrammes UML Jour du livrable 1 : Livraison d’EasySave v1.0 Documentation associée 🔹 Livrable 2 – EasySave v2.0 & v1.1 (non évalué) Lendemain du livrable 1 : Cahiers des charges v2.0 et v1.1 Veille du livrable 2 : Livraison des diagrammes UML Jour du livrable 2 : Livraison des versions 🔹 Livrable 3 – EasySave v3.0 Lendemain du livrable 2 : Cahier des charges v3.0 Avant-veille soutenance : Livraison des diagrammes UML Veille soutenance : Livraison d’EasySave v3.0 Jour de soutenance : Soutenance du projet 💼 Positionnement Produit – Suite ProSoft EasySave est un logiciel professionnel intégré à la politique tarifaire ProSoft. 💰 Prix unitaire : 200 € HT 🛠️ Contrat de maintenance annuel : 5j/7 – 8h à 17h Mises à jour incluses 12 % du prix d’achat Tacite reconduction avec revalorisation indexée SYNTEC 📚 Documentation attendue Le projet inclut obligatoirement : 👤 Documentation utilisateur Manuel d’utilisation sur une seule page 🧑‍💻 Documentation support technique Emplacement par défaut du logiciel Configuration minimale requise Emplacement des fichiers de configuration Informations nécessaires au support client 📝 Autres documents Diagrammes UML (par version) Release notes obligatoires 🛠️ Outils & Technologies Environnement de développement Visual Studio 2022 ou supérieur GitHub (gestion du code et des documents) Draw.io (préconisé pour les diagrammes UML) ⚠️ Le tuteur/pilote doit être invité sur le dépôt GitHub. Langage & Framework Langage : C# Framework : .NET 8.0 ✅ Exigences de qualité Lisibilité & maintenabilité Code, commentaires et documents en anglais Fonctions de taille raisonnable Aucune duplication inutile de code Respect strict des conventions de nommage Architecture pensée pour l’évolution du produit Gestion des versions Versions majeures et mineures clairement identifiées Réduction maximale des coûts pour les futures évolutions Capacité de réaction rapide face aux dysfonctionnements Interface utilisateur Logiciel destiné à être distribué chez des clients IHM soignées et ergonomiques obligatoires 🚀 Objectifs globaux Développer un logiciel de sauvegarde fiable et évolutif Simuler un cycle de développement professionnel Produire une base logicielle réutilisable par d’autres équipes Respecter les standards industriels de qualité logicielle 📦 Statut du projet 📌 Projet en cours – versions livrées selon le calendrier pédagogique.

api/CryptoSoft.FileManager.html.title: Class FileManager | EasySaveDocs
api/CryptoSoft.FileManager.html.summary: Class FileManager Namespace CryptoSoft Assembly CryptoSoft.dll Provides logic for file encryption and decryption using XOR operations. Ensures single-instance execution across the system using a global Mutex. public class FileManager Inheritance object FileManager Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Examples Usage example: if (FileManager.IsSingleInstance) { // This is the only instance, proceed with encryption double timeTaken = FileManager.CryptFile("path/to/source.txt", "path/to/dest.txt", "encryptionKey"); Console.WriteLine($"File encrypted in {timeTaken} ms"); } else { // Another instance is already running, wait for it to finish Console.WriteLine("Another instance is running. Waiting..."); FileManager.WaitForInstance(); Console.WriteLine("Previous instance finished. You can now run the encryption."); } Properties IsSingleInstance Gets a value indicating whether this process is the only instance (true), or if another instance already holds the encryption mutex (false). public static bool IsSingleInstance { get; } Property Value bool Methods CryptFile(string, string, string) Encrypts (or decrypts) a source file and writes the result to a destination path using an XOR cipher. public static double CryptFile(string sourcePath, string destPath, string key) Parameters sourcePath string The absolute or relative path to the file to be encrypted. destPath string The absolute or relative path where the encrypted file will be saved. key string The encryption key used for the XOR operation. Returns double The time taken to complete the encryption process in milliseconds. Returns -1 if the source file does not exist. ReleaseInstance() Releases the global mutex if this process created and owns it. Note: This is optional as the mutex will be automatically released upon process exit. public static void ReleaseInstance() WaitForInstance() Blocks the current thread and waits until the global mutex is released by another running instance. public static void WaitForInstance()

api/EasyLog.IConfig.html.summary: Interface IConfig Namespace EasyLog Assembly EasyLog.dll Defines the configuration contract required by the logging system. Any class implementing this interface must provide paths, formats, and networking details for the logger. public interface IConfig Methods getBoolLogsOnLocal() Checks whether the application is configured to persist logs locally on the machine. bool getBoolLogsOnLocal() Returns bool true if local logging is enabled; otherwise, false. getBoolLogsOnServer() Checks whether the application is configured to send logs over the network to a central server. bool getBoolLogsOnServer() Returns bool true if server logging is enabled; otherwise, false. getLogDirectoryPath() Retrieves the directory path where local daily log files are stored. string getLogDirectoryPath() Returns string A string representing the log directory path. getLogRealTimeFile() Retrieves the specific file path used to track the real-time state of ongoing tasks. string getLogRealTimeFile() Returns string A string representing the real-time log file path. getLogsFormat() Retrieves the current global formatting style used for generated logs (e.g., "JSON" or "XML"). string getLogsFormat() Returns string A string representing the log format. getServerIp() Retrieves the target IP address of the centralized logging server. string getServerIp() Returns string A string representing the server's IP address. getServerPort() Retrieves the target port number used to connect to the centralized logging server. int getServerPort() Returns int An integer representing the server's port. loadConfigFile() Loads or reloads the configuration settings from the underlying physical file (e.g., a JSON config file). void loadConfigFile() setLogsFormat(string) Updates the formatting style used for future log entries. void setLogsFormat(string newLogsFormat) Parameters newLogsFormat string The new format to apply (e.g., "JSON" or "XML"). setServerIp(string) Updates the IP address of the remote centralized logging server. void setServerIp(string newServerIp) Parameters newServerIp string The new server IP address. setServerPort(int) Updates the port number of the remote centralized logging server. void setServerPort(int newServerPort) Parameters newServerPort int The new server port number.

api/EasyLog.Logger.html.title: Class Logger | EasySaveDocs
api/EasyLog.Logger.html.summary: Class Logger Namespace EasyLog Assembly EasyLog.dll Handles the generation, formatting, and dispatching of application logs. Supports writing to local files and streaming to a remote centralized logging server via TCP sockets. Implements the Singleton pattern to ensure a single logging stream across the application. public class Logger Inheritance object Logger Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods formatCompleteSaveMessage(string, string, string, string) Formats the summary data of a fully completed backup job into a dictionary structure suitable for logging. public static Dictionary<string, string> formatCompleteSaveMessage(string name, string source, string target, string time) Parameters name string The name of the backup job. source string The root source directory. target string The root target directory. time string The timestamp of completion. Returns Dictionary<string, string> A dictionary containing the structured data. formatErrMessage(string) Formats an application exception or error message into a dictionary structure suitable for logging. public static Dictionary<string, string> formatErrMessage(string errorMessage) Parameters errorMessage string The raw error string or exception message. Returns Dictionary<string, string> A dictionary containing the error string and a timestamp. formatInfoRealTimeMessage(string, string, string, string, dynamic) Formats the raw data of a real-time state change into a dictionary structure suitable for logging. public static Dictionary<string, string> formatInfoRealTimeMessage(string name, string source, string target, string time, dynamic saveTaskState) Parameters name string The name of the backup job. source string The source file path. target string The target file path. time string The timestamp of the event. saveTaskState dynamic The dynamic state of the task (e.g., RUNNING, PAUSED). Returns Dictionary<string, string> A dictionary containing the structured data. formatLogMessage(string, string, string, int, double, string) Formats the raw data of a completed file transfer into a dictionary structure suitable for logging. public static Dictionary<string, string> formatLogMessage(string name, string source, string target, int size, double transferTime, string time) Parameters name string The name of the backup job. source string The source file path. target string The target file path. size int The size of the transferred file in bytes. transferTime double The time taken to copy the file in milliseconds. time string The timestamp of the completion. Returns Dictionary<string, string> A dictionary containing the structured data. getCurrentFormat() Gets the current LogFormat enum value being used by the logger. public LogFormat getCurrentFormat() Returns LogFormat The active log format. getInstance(IConfig) Retrieves the thread-safe singleton instance of the Logger. public static Logger getInstance(IConfig config) Parameters config IConfig The configuration provider to use if the logger needs to be initialized. Returns Logger The single active instance of the Logger. getLogsFormat() Retrieves the current format as a string (useful for UI data binding). public string getLogsFormat() Returns string A string representation of the format (e.g., "Json" or "Xml"). isConnectedToServer() Checks whether the logger currently has an active TCP connection to the remote server. public bool isConnectedToServer() Returns bool true if the socket is connected; otherwise, false. log(Dictionary<string, string>) Records a historical log entry. It appends the log to a local daily file and/or sends it to the remote server, depending on the active configuration settings. public bool log(Dictionary<string, string> message) Parameters message Dictionary<string, string> A dictionary containing the key-value pairs representing the log data. Returns bool true if the log was successfully recorded/sent everywhere it was supposed to go; otherwise, false. logRealTime(Dictionary<string, string>) Records a real-time log entry by completely overwriting the target file. This is used to maintain a constant "current state" of the application for external monitoring tools. public bool logRealTime(Dictionary<string, string> message) Parameters message Dictionary<string, string> A dictionary containing the state data. Returns bool true if the file was successfully overwritten; otherwise, false. reconnectToServer() Forces a disconnection and attempts to establish a new connection to the remote logging server. public bool reconnectToServer() Returns bool true if the reconnection was successful; otherwise, false. setCurrentFormat(LogFormat) Sets the LogFormat enum and persists the change to the global configuration file. public void setCurrentFormat(LogFormat format) Parameters format LogFormat The new LogFormat to apply. setLogsFormat(string) Attempts to parse a string input into a LogFormat enum and updates the logger. public void setLogsFormat(string format) Parameters format string A string representing the format ("json" or "xml", case-insensitive).

index.html.summary: Welcome to EasySave Documentation EasySave is a robust, lightweight, and scalable backup management software designed to secure your data efficiently. Whether you need full daily backups or quick differential saves, EasySave provides a reliable and modular solution tailored to your needs. Key Features Smart Backup Strategies: Choose between Complete (full copy) and Differential (only modified files) backup modes to optimize your storage and time. Real-Time & Daily Logging: Track every file transfer and system state in real-time. Logs can be formatted in JSON or XML and stored locally or sent to a centralized remote server via TCP. On-the-Fly Encryption: Seamlessly encrypt specific file extensions using the integrated CryptoSoft XOR encryption module. Business Software Detection: Automatically pauses resource-heavy backups when critical business applications are launched to preserve system performance. Large File Handling: Prioritizes file extensions and limits concurrent large file processing using advanced semaphore logic to prevent disk bottlenecking. Multilingual Support: Fully dynamic UI supporting multiple languages (English, French) via a dedicated LanguageService. Architecture Overview EasySave is built using the MVVM (Model-View-ViewModel) architectural pattern in C# / WPF, ensuring a clean separation of concerns: Model: Handles the core business logic, file manipulation, state management, and strategy execution (CompleteSave, DifferentialSave, SaveSpace). ViewModel: Acts as the reactive bridge, managing UI states (SaveJobState), translations, and asynchronous background monitoring. View: A modern WPF-based graphical interface (and a functional CLI) for smooth user interactions. External Libraries: Employs modular internal libraries such as EasyLog for logging management and CryptoSoft for cryptography.
